questions.question: What are the three levels of classification for national security information?
options: Confidential, Secret, and Top Secret
Sensitive, Secret, and Top Secret
Restricted, Confidential, and Secret
For Official Use Only, Secret, and Top Secret

questions.question: Which classification level is applied to information whose unauthorized disclosure could reasonably be expected to cause exceptionally grave damage to national security?
options: Secret
Top Secret
Confidential
Sensitive Compartmented Information

questions.question: Which classification level is applied to information whose unauthorized disclosure could reasonably be expected to cause serious damage to national security?
options: Confidential
Secret
Controlled Unclassified Information
Top Secret

questions.question: Which classification level is applied to information whose unauthorized disclosure could reasonably be expected to cause damage to national security?
options: Secret
Top Secret
Confidential
For Official Use Only

questions.question: Who has the authority to make original classification decisions for national security information?
options: Any government employee with a security clearance
Any military officer at the rank of O-4 or above
Only the Director of National Intelligence
Only individuals specifically designated in writing by the President, Vice President, or agency heads

questions.question: What is derivative classification?
options: The initial determination that information requires protection against unauthorized disclosure

The incorporating, paraphrasing, restating, or generating in new form information that is already classified, and marking the newly developed material consistent with the classification markings of the source
The reclassification of information from a lower to a higher classification level
A process used to declassify information after a set period

questions.question: What document provides guidance for derivative classifiers on how to classify specific elements of information?
options: A Security Classification Guide (SCG)
A Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)
A Concept of Operations (CONOPS)
An Operations Plan (OPLAN)

questions.question: How often must individuals who perform derivative classification receive training?
options: Annually
Every 2 years
Every 5 years
Only once upon initial appointment

questions.question: Which marking is required on every classified document to indicate the overall classification level?
options: A classification advisory on the last page only
A banner line at the top and bottom of each page showing the highest classification level of information on that page
A footer on each page with the originator's name
A watermark visible only under ultraviolet light

questions.question: What is the purpose of portion markings on a classified document?
options: To identify the author of each section
To track the number of copies distributed
To mark the document for automatic declassification after 10 years
To indicate the classification level of each portion (paragraph, subject line, title, etc.) of the document

questions.question: What is the standard maximum duration for classification before information must be reviewed for declassification?
options: 5 years
25 years
15 years
10 years

questions.question: What is the minimum level of security container required for storing Top Secret information?
options: A GSA-approved security container or an approved vault or secure room meeting specific standards
A locked desk drawer in a secured building
Any commercially available safe with a combination lock
A standard filing cabinet with a bar lock

questions.question: When classified material is not in use or under direct supervision, what must be done with it?
options: It must be stored in a GSA-approved security container, vault, or secure room
It may be left on a desk if the office door is locked
It should be placed in a personal briefcase for safekeeping
It may be stored in any container that has a padlock

questions.question: What type of investigation is typically required for a Top Secret security clearance?
options: National Agency Check with Inquiries (NACI)
Access National Agency Check and Inquiries (ANACI)
A Tier 5 investigation (formerly known as a Single Scope Background Investigation)
A basic criminal history check

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the adjudicative guidelines considered when determining security clearance eligibility?
options: Allegiance to the United States
Financial considerations
Physical fitness test scores
Foreign influence

questions.question: What is the Personnel Security program primarily designed to ensure?
options: That only trustworthy individuals are granted access to classified information and sensitive positions
That all military members receive annual security training
That physical security measures are installed at all military facilities
That all employees receive a Top Secret clearance upon hiring

questions.question: What is the Industrial Security program's primary purpose?
options: To regulate the sale of military surplus equipment
To safeguard classified information released to or generated by contractors working on government contracts
To manage construction contracts on military installations
To inspect commercial manufacturing facilities for safety compliance

questions.question: What is the first step of the Operations Security (OPSEC) five-step process?
options: Analyze threats
Identify critical information
Apply countermeasures
Assess risk

questions.question: What is the correct order of the five steps of the OPSEC process?
options: Analyze threats, identify critical information, assess risk, analyze vulnerabilities, apply countermeasures
Identify critical information, analyze threats, analyze vulnerabilities, assess risk, apply countermeasures
Apply countermeasures, assess risk, analyze threats, analyze vulnerabilities, identify critical information
Assess risk, identify critical information, analyze threats, apply countermeasures, analyze vulnerabilities

questions.question: In OPSEC, what is 'critical information'?
options: Any information that has been classified at the Secret level or above
All operational orders issued by a commander
Only intelligence reports marked as time-sensitive
Information about friendly activities, intentions, capabilities, or limitations that an adversary needs to plan and act effectively against friendly mission accomplishment

questions.question: In the OPSEC process, what does the 'analyze vulnerabilities' step involve?
options: Examining friendly actions and open-source information to identify what could reveal critical information to adversaries
Determining the cost of potential security upgrades
Counting the number of security incidents in the previous quarter
Reviewing all classified documents for proper marking

questions.question: What is Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)?
options: Information that has been declassified from Secret
A type of classification between Confidential and Unclassified
Government-created or -possessed information that requires safeguarding or dissemination controls pursuant to law, regulation, or government-wide policy, but is not classified
Information that is publicly available through Freedom of Information Act requests

questions.question: What type of security incident involves the loss, compromise, or suspected compromise of classified information?
options: An administrative discrepancy
A physical security deviation
A personnel security concern
A security incident (formerly called a security violation or infraction)

questions.question: When a security incident involving classified information is discovered, what is the first action that should be taken?
options: Protect the classified material from further compromise and immediately report the incident to the security manager and commander
Wait until the next duty day to report it through the chain of command
Conduct a personal investigation to determine what happened before reporting
Send an email to the information owner describing the incident

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the DoD Insider Threat Program?
options: To monitor all internet activity of government employees
To conduct annual polygraph examinations on all cleared personnel
To restrict social media use by military members
To deter, detect, and mitigate threats posed by individuals who have or had authorized access to DoD resources and use that access to harm national security

questions.question: Which of the following behaviors might be a potential indicator of an insider threat?
options: Taking authorized leave during holiday periods
Volunteering for extra duty assignments
Unexplained affluence, working unusual hours without authorization, or attempts to access information outside one's need to know
Attending professional military education courses

questions.question: What is Continuous Evaluation (CE) in the context of personnel security?
options: An ongoing vetting process that reviews relevant criminal, financial, and other data between periodic reinvestigations to assess continued eligibility for access
A one-time comprehensive background investigation conducted every 10 years
A monthly physical fitness evaluation for security forces personnel
A requirement for cleared personnel to take a polygraph examination every year

questions.question: What is the purpose of cybersecurity awareness training for military members?
options: To qualify members to work in cybersecurity career fields
To ensure personnel understand their responsibilities in protecting information systems and recognizing cyber threats such as phishing, malware, and social engineering
To teach members advanced network penetration testing techniques
To certify members as information system security managers

questions.question: Which of the following is a common social engineering technique used to compromise security?
options: Encrypting all files on a shared drive
Conducting a vulnerability scan of the network
Implementing two-factor authentication
Phishing — sending deceptive emails designed to trick users into revealing sensitive information or clicking malicious links

questions.question: What should a user do if they suspect they have received a phishing email on a government network?
options: Forward it to colleagues so they can verify if it is legitimate
Click the link to see where it leads before reporting
Delete it immediately without telling anyone
Report it to the Information Assurance Officer or help desk and do not click any links or open attachments

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of physical security measures on a military installation?
options: To safeguard personnel, equipment, installations, and information from unauthorized access, espionage, sabotage, and other threats
To control vehicle traffic flow for convenience
To provide aesthetic improvements to the installation perimeter
To generate revenue through access badge fees

questions.question: What does Antiterrorism (AT) awareness include?
options: Only the identification of explosive devices
Exclusively the training of security forces to conduct counterterrorism raids
Only the management of bomb threat evacuation procedures
Understanding threat levels, personal protective measures, and recognizing indicators of potential terrorist activity

questions.question: What is Force Protection Condition (FPCON) designed to communicate?
options: The physical fitness standards required for deployment
The weather conditions affecting military operations
A progressive level of protective measures to be taken when a terrorist threat is assessed against military personnel and facilities
The budget allocation for security improvements at an installation

questions.question: What are the Force Protection Condition (FPCON) levels, from lowest to highest threat?
options: Low, Moderate, High, Critical
Green, Yellow, Orange, Red
Normal, Alpha, Bravo, Charlie, Delta
Level 1, Level 2, Level 3, Level 4, Level 5

questions.question: What FPCON level applies when a general global threat of possible terrorist activity exists and warrants a routine security posture?
options: FPCON Alpha
FPCON Bravo
FPCON Delta
FPCON Normal

questions.question: What does FPCON Delta indicate?
options: A general threat of possible terrorist activity
An increased and more predictable threat of terrorist activity
A terrorist attack has occurred or intelligence indicates imminent terrorist action against a specific location or personnel
A slightly elevated general threat of terrorist activity

questions.question: What is the 'need to know' principle in information security?
options: A determination by the holder of classified information that a prospective recipient requires access to specific classified information to perform official duties
A requirement that all classified information be shared with every cleared person in an organization
A policy requiring supervisors to know the details of every project in their organization
A rule that classified information must be shared with allied foreign governments upon request

questions.question: What two conditions must be met before a person may access classified information?
options: They must be a U.S. citizen and an officer
They must be on active duty and hold a Top Secret clearance
They must hold the appropriate security clearance level and have a valid need to know
They must be assigned to an intelligence unit and have completed SERE training

questions.question: What is the Information Protection program responsible for?
options: Only the protection of classified national security information
Managing personnel fitness evaluations
The integrated safeguarding of classified information, CUI, and other protected information across all security disciplines
Exclusively monitoring cybersecurity threats to Air Force networks

questions.question: In the OPSEC process, what does the 'apply countermeasures' step involve?
options: Identifying the critical information that needs protection
Determining which adversary nations are the greatest threat
Briefing the commander on historical security incidents
Implementing measures to eliminate vulnerabilities, minimize risks, or deny adversaries the ability to exploit critical information
